★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.45 4.46 2.99 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.50 0.78 0.44 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.60 0.99 0.52 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.34 2.69 2.03 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.11 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-04 75 37 43 583 1 583
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-09-28 64 64 0 757 3 1,287
Total weighted health score
759.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (167)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

5 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
158 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0572 Give residents a notice of rights, rules, services and charges. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0790 Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0807 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides drinks consistent with resident needs and preferences and sufficient to maintain resident hydration. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2025-09-04 2025-11-10
